summary: Jeff Atwood critically examines Joel Spolsky's recent controversial decisions, particularly the revelation that FogBugz is written in Wasabi, a proprietary in-house language based on VBScript. Atwood argues that creating your own programming language is a 'toxic decision' that contradicts Joel's previous sensible advice. He further challenges Joel's claim that Ruby would be too slow for FogBugz, pointing out logical inconsistencies in using a language that compiles to VBScript while citing performance concerns. The post questions whether Joel, once a revered voice in software development, has lost his way.
key_points: Writing your own proprietary programming language is a dangerous decision that contradicts sound software development principles
Joel's performance arguments against Ruby are inconsistent since Wasabi compiles to VBScript, which isn't known for speed
Performance-critical code sections can be written in compiled languages and called from other languages - you don't need one language for everything
Rejecting an entire language based on one small performance-critical section violates the 90/10 rule of optimization
Even respected industry figures can make decisions that seem to contradict their own established wisdom
money_quotes: Writing your own language is absolutely beyond the pale.
It's a toxic decision that is so completely at odds with Joel's previous excellent and sane advice on software development that people literally thought he was joking.
You couldn't possibly have heard it, but that was the sound of fifty thousand programmers' heads simultaneously exploding.
Never mind the fact that your company's flagship application is still written in a freaky custom language based on VBScript that only three people in the world know how to program.
I understand the philosophy that developer cycles are more important than cpu cycles, but frankly that's just a bumper-sticker slogan and not fair to the people who are complaining about performance.
Is there some law that states all code for a web application must be in the same exact language?
I reject this new, highly illogical Joel Spolsky. I demand the immediate return of the sage, sane, wise Joel Spolsky of years past.
key_insight: Even the most respected voices in software development can make decisions that contradict their own advice, and the community should critically evaluate ideas based on merit rather than the reputation of who proposes them.
